source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A rigid, insulated vessel contains superheated vapor steam at 3 MPa, 400\circ\text{C}. A valve on the vessel is opened, allowing steam to escape. The overall process is irreversible, but the steam remaining inside the vessel goes through a reversible adiabatic expansion. Determine the fraction of steam that has escaped when the final state inside is saturated vapor.

Determine the fraction of steam that has escaped when the final state inside is saturated vapor.
